text: The names ‘Egypt’ and ‘Egyptian’ have, as before, been inserted to make the sense of the original plainer. The same is to be said of the following words: ‘frogs’ in verse 3, ‘brazen serpent’ in verse 7, and ‘manna’ in verse 27.

vv. 22, 23: The meaning here is not very clearly expressed; it is, that fire twice failed to produce its natural effect, once when it did not melt the hailstones which fell in Egypt, and again when it did not melt the manna in the cooking-pots of the Israelites (see verse 27 below, and Ex. 16.21).
